text: Working with APIs is an essential skill for modern developers. APIs communicate through HTTP requests, such as GET to retrieve data or POST to send data. The data is usually formatted in JSON, which is lightweight and easy to read. Most APIs require authentication, often using an API key, to ensure secure access. When using APIs, you must also respect rate limits, which control how many requests you can make in a given time. If you exceed these limits, your access may be temporarily blocked. Finally, always implement error handling to manage failed requests gracefully. This makes your code more reliable and user-friendly.
